title: Skills plus MCP Integration and 2026 Evolution · Skills and MCP Review Board
summary: Fresh skill design, trigger, packaging, MCP protocol, transport, schema, client, authorization, threat, and integration cases with build tasks. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope.
Use a plugin to distribute skills, connectors, hooks, and presentation assets as one installable unit.
Route the agent to only the references or scripts needed for the current variant.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

# Add typed, bounded protocol increments below.
starterFiles.config:
# Skill and MCP integration portfolio
# Record scope, triggers, permissions, tests, and maintenance evidence.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Scope Layers evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Scope Layers assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext integration capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Scope Layers, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production skill and MCP integration package.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: All recurring agent behavior belongs in a skill. This fails the Scope Layers contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Claude Code MCP (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls Claude Code MCP transports, scopes, commands, trust prompts, resources, and prompts.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: All recurring agent behavior belongs in a skill.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise
